title: Wealthy consumers are turning to jewelry as an investment, especially colored gemstones
summary: Lofty gold prices and market volatility are pushing wealthy consumers toward jewelry, especially rare colored gemstones, as handbags lose appeal.

title: Nuclear in the Spotlight amid Oil, Gas Crunch
summary: Nuclear energy has been making a comeback recently as one of the low-carbon baseload generation options. title: Big Oil Flocks to Alaska in Record-Setting Petroleum Lease Sale
summary: The first lease sale in the National Petroleum Reserve-Alaska in seven years became the most successful auction in the area ever, as oil majors bid on hundreds of tracts, signaling they haven’t given up on Alaska’s petroleum resources despite development and court challenges. title: SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce on ETFs: 'We want to work with people on new products'
summary: SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce indicates an openness to work with Wall Street on fresh exchange-traded fund products tied to cryptocurrencies and tokenization.

title: Opinion: As Trump eyes Cuba, my trips there a decade ago remind me how different things were
summary: Trump has threatened a 'friendly takeover' of Cuba after cutting it off from Venezuela's oil. CNBC's Justin Solomon reflects how different things used to be.

title: Small cap-focused Russell 2000 becomes first U.S. benchmark to enter correction territory
summary: Small caps are especially sensitive to changes in oil prices and a slowdown in the economic cycle.

title: David Zaslav’s WBD-Paramount deal payout highlights new 'golden parachutes' for CEOs
summary: WBD CEO David Zaslav could see a payout of more than $800 million after the Paramount Skydance deal.

title: Norway’s Output Holds Steady—but Spare Capacity Is Gone
summary: Norwegian oil production slipped 0.2 % in February as output fell slightly by 3,000 barrels per day (bpd) compared to January, averaging 1.97 million bpd, according to preliminary figures from the Norwegian Offshore Directorate. title: US Drillers Add Oil Rigs For Second Week In A Row As Prices Soar
summary: The total number of active drilling rigs for oil and gas in the United States fell this week, according to new data that Baker Hughes published on Friday, bringing the total rig count in the US to 552 this week, down 41 from this same time last year. title: Oil Prices Ease As US Pulls Out All Stops To Secure Supply
summary: Oil prices pulled back Friday, but not because the market suddenly feels safe. This is more of a tactical breather than a trend change.
